title: -Reserving Predevelopment Funds for the Development of Affordable Housing on the Ashby BART West Lot

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution to:
1. Reserve $2 million for Resources for Community Development (RCD) to support
the development of affordable housing at Ashby BART West Lot.
2. Authorize the City Manager or designee to execute all original or amended
documents or agreements to effectuate this action.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
On April 27, 2021, the Berkeley City Council unanimously adopted Resolution 69,833-
N.S. which, in part, provisionally reserved $53 million of City controlled funds as the
subsidy needed to achieve at least 35% affordable housing at the North Berkeley and
Ashby BART Station sites and allowed for a portion of the funding to be allocated to
predevelopment uses. In accordance with Resolution No. 69,833-N.S., the $53 million
reserve fund is or shall be funded with $40 million in bond funds pursuant to Measure O
passed by Berkeley voters on November 6, 2018, and $13 million in Affordable Housing
Mitigation Fees. $18.5 million of the total BART funds are available to support affordable
housing on the Ashby BART West Lot. This current request for $2 million will come from
Affordable Housing Mitigation Fees (Fund 120) and will be recommended for approval
through the annual appropriations ordinance process.
sections.current_situation:
BART issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) on December 3rd, 2024 to select a team to
develop housing at the Ashby BART station’s West Lot. Through the RFP, the City
offered up to $1M in predevelopment funding to encourage nonprofit participation.
Two development teams responded to the RFP, including Adeline Alliance Partners, a
team comprised of RCD, Strategic Urban Development Alliance, Relequity Enterprises,
and The Pacific Companies. RCD applied in May 2025 for the predevelopment funding,
requesting that the City enter into a loan for up to $1 million to support West Lot
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 6
Predevelopment Loan for Ashby BART West Lot CONSENT CALENDAR
September 9, 2025
predevelopment activities. These funds would be used to pay for the cost of the RCD-
planned affordable housing developments which represent two of the planned
developments on the site, as well as RCD’s share of master planning costs. RCD has
subsequently increased their request to $2 million to support predevelopment activities
as they anticipate needing approximately $2.7 million. RCD is committed to pursuing
other funding sources to fill the remaining gap of $700,000. Although this request is
higher than the parties’ expectations as articulated in Section 7.1.1 of the exchange
agreement, staff support the higher request, and will work with RCD to identify
milestones for disbursement of the City predevelopment funding, which will be
incorporated into the City’s loan agreement.
A selection panel with representatives from the City and BART reviewed the
qualifications of both teams, interviewed the team leads, and attended the public
presentations on May 30, 2025. Based on their cumulative score, the panel selected the
Adeline Alliance Partners, and the BART Board of Supervisors authorized entering into
an Exclusive Negotiating Agreement (ENA)
sections.background:
For the past few years, the City has been working closely with BART on the
development of the North Berkeley and Ashby BART sites. The two entities entered into
a Memorandum of Understanding in March 2020 that provided a framework for the
collaboration, including processes for public participation and an estimated timeline for
selecting developers. The MOU was amended in December 2020 to reflect schedule
delays due to the pandemic. The City created a BART Community Advisory Group
(CAG), made up of 15 members of the public to develop a vision for affordable housing,
land use, station access, building form, and new public spaces. The CAG met
throughout 2020 and 2021 at public meetings and community workshops, and provided
Page 2
Page 3 of 6
Predevelopment Loan for Ashby BART West Lot CONSENT CALENDAR
September 9, 2025
feedback on the new zoning regulations impacting the BART sites. The City and BART
entered into a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) in June 2022 to address additional
aspects of the collaboration.
The CAG was instrumental in shaping the City - BART Joint Vision and Priorities for
Transit-Oriented Development for Ashby and North Berkeley Stations (JVP). The JVP
articulates the shared goals of the City and BART, including the goal of achieving at
least 35% affordability at each site.
In 2024, BART and the City approved an exchange agreement, agreeing to exchange
the City’s air rights over the West Lot for ownership of the East Lot. This agreement was
based on the JVP, the CAG, additional community input, and extensive economic and
technical analysis. The City and BART signed a real estate agreement for Ashby BART
outlining project requirements for affordable housing and other community benefits.
The City typically provides two types of loans through the Housing Trust Fund: 1)
predevelopment loans that are short-term (five years) and allow developers to assess
project feasibility to better position the project to pursue competitive funding at the state
level; a
sections.rationale:
There are early and significant costs associated with transforming the current parking lot
at the Ashby BART station to a vibrant residential community serving a mix of incomes
and household types. RCD will need to pay its share of BART’s ENA fees, station
access fees, and other costs related to master planning, infrastructure, and
predevelopment work. There are few sources of predevelopment funding available, and
most come with interest rates higher than what the City offers. Staff recommend
increasing the amount from $1 million to $2 million, with conditions of disbursement
outlined in the predevelopment loan agreement to ensure that RCD is meeting specific
milestones before the City funds are disbursed. This increase shifts $1 million from the
development to predevelopment phase, but the total commitment to the project remains
the same.
sections.alternatives:
Since the City agreed in the Ashby West Lot RFP to make this funding available for the
selected nonprofit developer, and since RCD qualifies for this funding under the City’s
Page 3
Page 4 of 6
Predevelopment Loan for Ashby BART West Lot CONSENT CALENDAR
September 9, 2025
Housing Trust Fund criteria, staff did not consider any alternative actions. If the City
chooses not to support a predevelopment loan, or to support a loan for the originally
requested amount of $1 million, the RCD team would need to secure other sources of
financing, which may have less favorable loan terms than the City’s predevelopment
loan. This would add costs to the project, and make it more expensive to deliver new
affordable housing units.
CONTACT PERSON
Kelly deWolfe, Community Development Project Coordinator, HHCS, 510-981-5424

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
RESERVING AFFORDABLE HOUSING MITIGATION FEES FOR
PREDEVELOPMENT AT THE ASHBY WEST BART SITE
WHEREAS, the City Council established a Housing Trust Fund Program (HTF) to guide
the City’s work to assist in the development and expansion of housing affordable to low-
and moderate-income persons who either work or reside within the City of Berkeley (City),
and authorized the City Manager to implement the Program; and
WHEREAS, there is a great need for affordable and special needs housing in the City of
Berkeley as stated in the General Plan Housing Element and the City of Berkeley’s
Consolidated plan; and
WHEREAS, the City and BART are collaborating on the development of the Ashby BART
West Lot Site, and articulated shared goals in a Memorandum of Understanding,
Memorandum of Agreement, Joint Vision and Priorities for Transit-Oriented
Development, and Exchange Agreement for Ashby and North Berkeley Stations; and
WHEREAS, the City and BART are committed to achieving a minimum of 35% affordable
housing at each BART site; and
WHEREAS, on April 27, 2021, with Resolution 69,883-N.S., the City Council reserved
$53 million, to be divided between the two BART sites, as the subsidy needed to achieve
35% affordability, and allowed for a portion of that funding to be considered for
predevelopment costs associated with affordable housing; and
WHEREAS, the $53 million includes $40 million in Measure O funds, and $13 million in
Affordable Housing Mitigation Fees; and
WHEREAS, in December 2024, BART issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) to select a
team to develop new housing at the Ashby West BART site and created an RFP selection
panel with representation from the City and BART; and
WHEREAS, two teams responded to the RFP, including Adeline Alliance Partners, a team
made up of Resources for Community Development, Strategic Urban Development
Alliance, Relequity Enterprises, and The Pacific Companies; and
